description: A color outdoor photograph of the Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building in Washington, D.C. A woman in a bright red pantsuit (apparently Labor Secretary) stands alone on the empty plaza/steps in front of the building. The brutalist concrete facade is decorated with a very large American flag hung vertically in the center and two enormous portrait banners flanking it: at left a banner showing Frances Perkins(?) and at right another historical figure, both bearing 'AMERICA 250' branding and the slogan 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'. While the building banners are themselves designed propaganda, the tweeted image itself is a candid architectural/event photograph rather than a self-contained designed meme graphic; OCR detected no overlaid graphic text. Messaging celebrates the 'America First' labor agenda tied to the America250 semiquincentennial. Note: the banner portraits are too small/oblique to identify with certainty.
notable_text: AMERICA 250; AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST (on building banners)
